title: palura chinensis f. leucocarpa
intro: Palura chinensis f. leucocarpa, also known as white-fruited palura, is a species of plant in the genus Palura, belonging to the family Rutaceae. It is a type of citrus tree native to China, and its fruits and seeds are used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Palura chinensis f. leucocarpa include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The plant's fruits and seeds can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or dried powders for medicinal use, or used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments.
